repairs.corrected_token: Motse said : All the rulers in the world desire their states to be wealthy, their people to be many, and their government and jurisdiction to be orderly.

repairs.raw_token: On the other hand, it desires people having energy to work for each other, those knowing the way to teach each other, These are the two opening lines of the ode “ Hsiao Ming ” in Shih Ching.

Who are those that love a Compare this them© of Motse’s with the foUowmg from Seneca, On Benefits, iv, 6 :  We can he thankful to a friend for a few acres, or a little money ; and yet for the freedom and command of the whole earth, and for the great benefits of our being, as life, health, and reason, we look upon ourselves as under no obligation.
repairs.corrected_token: Who are those that love the people and benefit the people, obey the will of Heaven and obtain reward from Heaven?

repairs.raw_token: If a man bestows upon us a house that is delicately beautified with painting, statues, gildings, and marbles, we make a mighty business of it, and yet it lies at the mercy of a pufi of wind, the snuff of a candle, and a hundred other accidents to lay it in the dust.

repairs.raw_token: [ The Confucianist says: Love among relations should depend upon the degree of relationship, and honour to the virtuous should be graded.2 This is to advocate a discrimination among the near and the distant relations and among the respectable and the humble.
repairs.corrected_token: The Confucianists say that love among relations should depend on the degree of relationship, and honour to the virtuous should be graded. This advocates discrimination among near and distant relations and among the respectable and the humble.
